summary: Re-ran the 3 previously-failing AI explain tests from iter_109 after EMERGENT_LLM_KEY was restored to /app/backend/.env. All 3 now PASS. Full file (9/9) is green. Quick regression sweep on iter_105/106/107 also still green (54 passed, 1 pre-existing design skip). Issue from iter_109 is fully resolved with no code changes needed beyond the env-var addition.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L3792-3793 _get_llm_key() still silently returns '' when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is missing — recommend raising 503 with a clear 'LLM key not configured' message instead of letting litellm AuthenticationError surface as opaque 500. Carried over from iter_109; not blocking but worth hardening for future ops.

success_rate.backend: 100% (9/9 on iter_109 file, 54/54 on iter_105/106/107 regression with 1 pre-existing design skip)

test_credentials: Used documented creds: Abhijit.patil/Admin@123 (admin), Gaurav.pandey/Student@123 (student). Both authenticate via POST /api/auth/login as expected.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created in this iteration. The 3 AI tests reuse the existing harness from iter_109.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter_110 was a verification-only run. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (sk-emergent-950A998B90c8f3f3aB) is confirmed present in /app/backend/.env. Both /api/ai/explain-standalone and /api/ai/explain-recommendation now return 200 with non-empty scientifically-grounded text. Institutional-indications prompt enrichment for known brand/system (server.py L3811-3817 and L3874-3883) is operational; unknown brand/system path also returns 200 (no exception when inst_block is empty). New POST /api/audit/safety-override endpoint and the access-logs RBAC remain green. Iter_109 is fully closed.
rca_of_the_issue: Resolved — iter_109's CRITICAL was solely due to missing EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env. Main agent restored the key from emergent_integrations_manager. Re-running the same test file (no test/code changes) shows 9/9 green and confirms the new institutional Indications/Features prompt block works on both the known-system happy path and the unknown-system safety path.
